channel.title: The Car Jury, News
channel.description: News and verdicts on the Indian car market from The Car Jury.

item.title: Battery-as-a-Service Cuts EV Sticker Prices, But Raises Lifetime Cost
item.description: Tata, Hyundai and Maruti are pushing Battery-as-a-Service to slash EV sticker prices, but the lifetime bill for the buyer is going up, not down.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Kia Syros EV Caught Undisguised in Bengaluru: Launch is Close
item.description: Kia Syros EV spotted completely undisguised during a TVC shoot in Bengaluru. Closed grille, boxy silhouette confirmed. Launch imminent.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Tata Sierra Jubilee Edition: Smart Sweetener, Not a Reason to Buy
item.description: Tata marks 50,000 Sierra sales with a Jubilee Edition on Smart+, Pure and Adventure trims, adding ROQ accessories and a few missing features.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Mercedes-AMG E 53 PHEV lands July 23: 585hp, 100km EV range, one very expensive science project
item.description: Mercedes-AMG launches the E 53 HYBRID 4MATIC+ in India on July 23 with a 3.0-litre six, 585hp combined and over 100km EV range.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Volkswagen's China Collapse Drags Global Sales to 4-Year Low: India Buyers Unaffected
item.description: Volkswagen's Q2 2026 deliveries fell 8.6 percent globally as China market shrank 20 percent. What it means for Indian VW buyers.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Hyundai June 2026 Sales: Venue Tops Chart, Creta Slips as Supplier Fire Hits Output
item.description: Hyundai India June 2026 sales fell 10% YoY to 39,635 units after a supplier fire. Venue overtook Creta as the brand's top-seller.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Tata Sierra Jubilee Edition: An Accessory Pack With A Fancy Name, From Rs 11.99 Lakh
item.description: Tata launches Sierra Jubilee Edition from Rs 11.99 lakh with three dealer-fit accessory packs. The Car Jury on whether it changes the Sierra's case.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Kia Syros EV: 520km Range, Rs 15-20 Lakh Price Puts Nexon EV On Notice
item.description: Kia Syros EV gets 42kWh and 51.4kWh batteries from the Carens Clavis EV, up to 520km ARAI range. Bookings open, launch expected this month.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Nissan Can Still Build Its Own Cars At Renault's Chennai Plant
item.description: Nissan confirms it can build vehicles on its own platforms at the Renault-run RNAIPL Chennai plant, despite exiting the manufacturing JV last year.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Kia Sorento Bookings Open at Rs 25,000: Flagship Hybrid SUV Lands in October
item.description: Kia has unofficially opened Sorento bookings in India at Rs 25,000. A 1.6 hybrid, three seating layouts, and an October 2026 launch are on the cards.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: MG Shows GO Hatch and Cyber SUV at Goodwood: Europe First, India Later
item.description: MG unveils the GO electric hatchback and Cyber SUV concepts at Goodwood 2026. Europe is the priority; India gets these later, if at all.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: VW Tayron Life at Rs 41.99 Lakh: Cheaper Tayron, But Still a Hard Sell
item.description: Volkswagen Tayron Life launches at Rs 41.99 lakh ex-showroom, a 5-seat, softer-styled sibling to the R-Line with the same engine. TCJ's verdict inside.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Centre Kills Pure Petrol and E10 Choice: E20 Is Now Your Only Option
item.description: The Centre has ruled out selling pure petrol, E10 and E20 side by side, locking E20 as India's default petrol blend. Here is what it means.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Centre Orders States To Crack Down On Fuel Adulteration Amid E20 Backlash
item.description: The Centre has directed states to take iron-hand action against fuel adulteration, separating pump-level lapses from the E20 petrol debate.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: MG's First India PHEV Could Be Called Hector Hawk, Debuts July 16
item.description: MG has trademarked the Hector Hawk name in India, likely for its first PHEV, a rebadged Wuling Starlight 560 debuting July 16.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Nissan Bets On Alliances For India: Smart Move, But Execution Is Everything
item.description: Nissan will lean on Renault, Dongfeng, Honda and Mitsubishi alliances to navigate regional rules, with Chennai and the Tekton SUV central to its India play.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Mahindra Hikes Prices Up To 2.7%: Second Bump This Year, EVs Mostly Spared
item.description: Mahindra raises prices on almost every ICE model by up to 2.7 percent from July 10, 2026. Scorpio N and most EVs escape the hike.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Nissan Tekton claims 19.4kpl: Duster's numbers with a Nissan badge
item.description: Nissan Tekton claims 17.8-19.4kpl across its 1.0 and 1.3 turbo-petrol engines, matching the Renault Duster almost to the decimal. Here is our take.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Nissan Tekton lands at ₹10.49 lakh: the C-SUV bet Nissan cannot afford to fumble
item.description: Nissan Tekton launches in India at Rs 10.49 lakh ex-showroom with two turbo-petrol engines and 17+ ADAS features. The Car Jury's verdict for buyers.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Nissan Tekton bags 5 stars at Bharat NCAP, matches Duster's crash score
item.description: The Nissan Tekton scores a full 5 stars at Bharat NCAP, matching the Renault Duster with 30.49/32 for adults and 45/49 for children.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Centre Puts Brakes On E25 Petrol: Rollout Still In Testing, Says Puri
item.description: Union Minister Hardeep Singh Puri says E25 petrol is still in testing and calls reports of an imminent rollout a misrepresentation.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: BMW M2 M xDrive Confirmed for India: AWD Arrives, But Purists Won't Care
item.description: BMW confirms the all-wheel-drive M2 M xDrive for India. Same 480hp straight-six, 0.3s quicker to 100kph, rear-biased AWD with a 2WD mode.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Tata Sierra EV On-Road Prices Land Between Rs 19.39-28.19 Lakh Across Metros
item.description: Tata Sierra EV on-road prices are out across Kolkata, Chennai and Mumbai. Range spans Rs 19.39 lakh to Rs 28.19 lakh. Here is what to pay.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: BMW India's H1 2026 record: EVs and long-wheelbase cars carry the load
item.description: BMW India delivered a record 9,075 cars in H1 2026, up 17 percent. EVs grew 78 percent and LWB models took 52 percent of sales.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Volkswagen Tayron gets a cheaper variant on July 10: about time
item.description: Volkswagen teases a lower-spec Tayron ahead of its July 10 India launch, below the Rs 46.99 lakh R-Line. Here is what changes and what to expect.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: EV Sales June 2026: Tata Doubles Down, Mahindra Charges Hard
item.description: India's EV retail sales crossed 31,800 units in June 2026, up 107.75% YoY. Tata leads with 12,187 units, Mahindra follows at 7,766.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Mercedes-Benz India Is Right: Blame Adulterated Fuel, Not Just E20
item.description: Mercedes-Benz India says adulterated fuel is being wrongly blamed on E20 petrol, and wants tighter enforcement at pumps. Our verdict on what buyers should do.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: 2026 Defender gets urban Vertex variant, Octa loses 95hp to emissions
item.description: Land Rover updates the Defender for 2026 with a new urban Vertex variant, a 6-seat 110, and a detuned Octa. India launch expected but unconfirmed.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Maruti Grand Vitara, Invicto July 2026 Discounts: Small Sweetener, Not a Reason to Buy
item.description: Maruti offers up to Rs 95,000 off the Grand Vitara and Rs 2.10 lakh off the Invicto in July 2026. The Car Jury's take on whether it's enough.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Nissan Tekton launches without Duster's hybrid: badge-engineered, and it shows
item.description: Nissan Tekton launches 9 July 2026 without the Renault Duster's 1.8 strong-hybrid. Only 1.0 and 1.3 turbo-petrols. TCJ's verdict inside.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: ARAI's 2021 Study Confirms It: E20 Chews Through Rubber Parts Built for E10
item.description: A resurfaced 2021 ARAI study confirms E20 degrades rubber and plastic fuel-system parts in E10-era cars. Metals are fine, elastomers aren't.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Mercedes India Is Right: Delhi's Rs 30 Lakh EV Cap Punishes Premium Buyers
item.description: Mercedes-Benz India says Delhi's Rs 30 lakh cap on EV incentives will slow premium EV uptake even as the CLA electric drives record sales.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Mahindra's 'Attack Mode' EV Strategy: Bold Talk, But The Cars Have To Deliver
item.description: Anand Mahindra invokes Formula E's 'Attack Mode' as Mahindra ramps EV output to 8,000 units a month. The Car Jury weighs the strategy.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: JSW MG and Spinny Team Up on Used EVs: Finally, Someone Owns the Battery Problem
item.description: JSW MG Motor partners with Spinny for certified pre-owned EVs across 13 cities, with battery health checks and residual value clarity.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Mercedes India posts record H1 2026: E-Class leads, EQS SUV tops EV chart
item.description: Mercedes-Benz India logged its best-ever H1 with 9,768 units in 2026. The E-Class stays king, the EQS SUV rules EV sales, and AMG jumped 50 percent.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Mercedes GLB EV confirmed for India: the compact luxury EV Audi and BMW should worry about
item.description: Mercedes-Benz confirms the new GLB EV for India. 85kWh battery, up to 631km WLTP range, 800V architecture and MMA platform. Here is our verdict.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Maruti And Toyota Say E20 Is Safe For Pre-2023 Cars. We Are Not Buying The Full Story
item.description: Maruti and Toyota say E20 petrol causes no damage to pre-2023 cars designed for E10. The Car Jury on what owners should actually watch for.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Mahindra Vision S Interior Spied: BE6 Steering Signals Sub-4m With Big Car Kit
item.description: Mahindra Vision S sub-4m SUV interior spied with BE6-style steering and modern cabin, hinting at a serious Nexon and Brezza rival for 2027.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Tata Sierra EV 75 RWD Makes Less Power Than 63 kWh: The Odd Math Explained
item.description: Tata's Sierra EV 75 kWh RWD makes 209 PS vs the 63 kWh's 238 PS. Tata says it's an intentional packaging call, not a spec sheet error.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: MG Windsor EV Chases 70% Localisation: Smart Move, But Buyers Should Still Wait
item.description: JSW MG Motor targets over 70% localisation on the Windsor EV after 75,000 sales in 21 months. Here is what it means for buyers eyeing the electric MPV.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: New Toyota Hilux Lands in India on 28 July: Tougher Face, Same Ladder Frame
item.description: Toyota confirms the 9th-gen Hilux for India on 28 July 2026 with a sharper front end, new tech and the same IMV ladder-frame underneath.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Tata Slashes Up To Rs 3.35 Lakh Off Its EVs: Inventory Clearance Before Sierra EV Push
item.description: Tata Motors is offering up to Rs 3.35 lakh off on Harrier EV, Nexon EV, Curvv EV, Punch EV and Tiago EV this July 2026. Here is our take.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Kia Teases Syros EV And Sorento Hybrid: The Electrified Push India Actually Needs
item.description: Kia India has teased both a BEV and an HEV, pointing to the Syros EV this month and a Sorento hybrid three-row SUV to follow.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Mercedes Workers Rally Against Cost Cuts: What It Means for Indian Buyers
item.description: Thousands of Mercedes workers protested cost-cutting plans in Germany, signalling wider trouble for the country's auto industry. Our take on the fallout.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Honda Elevate Gets Rs 2.15 Lakh Off In July 2026: Grab It Or Hold?
item.description: Honda Elevate carries benefits up to Rs 2.15 lakh in July 2026, the highest across Honda's lineup. The Car Jury's take on whether to sign now.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: June 2026 Sales: Tata Punch And Nexon Lock Top Two, Maruti Still Owns The Ten
item.description: Tata Punch and Nexon topped June 2026 passenger vehicle sales in India, but Maruti placed five models in the Top 10. TCJ's read.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Tata Harrier EV gets Rs 2.75 lakh off in July: grab it now
item.description: Tata is offering up to Rs 2.75 lakh off the Harrier EV and up to Rs 3.35 lakh off the Curvv EV in July 2026. Here is the full breakdown.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: World Car Awards Tops Global Visibility Rankings, But Does It Move Indian Metal?
item.description: World Car Awards named most visible auto awards programme for the 14th year running, reaching 49.3 crore people in 2025-26 across five continents.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Mahindra XEV 9e Cineluxe Adds Satin Desert Myst: A Third Colour for the Flagship EV
item.description: Mahindra adds a new Satin Desert Myst finish to the XEV 9e Cineluxe Edition, joining Satin Black and Satin White in the flagship EV's lineup.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Maruti's Kharkhoda plant goes live: 10 lakh cars a year is the real threat to rivals
item.description: Maruti Suzuki opens its 800-acre Kharkhoda plant in Haryana. Capacity will double to 10 lakh units. What it means for buyers and rivals.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)
